LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MICHAEL HUNTER, MD'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT MICHAEL HUNTER'S DEMURRER URGES THE COURT TO RESOLVE SEVERAL FACTUAL DISPUTES, LARGELY BASED ON JUDICIAL NOTICE OF OUT-OF-COURT STATEMENTS. THAT IS NOT THE PROPER ROLE OF A DEMURRER; RATHER, "PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGATIONS MUST BE ACCEPTED AS TRUE." (WEIL & BROWN, CALIFORNIA PRACTICE GUIDE: CIVIL PROCEDURE BEFORE TRIAL (TRG 2016) 7:44.) THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600, TEL. 415.551.5868, EMAILS: TORREANOM@AOL.COM/MTOREANO@SFTC.ORG. =(302/RBU)

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT MICHAEL HUNTER, MD'S MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED AS FOLLOWS: 1. ALL PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO REQUEST NOS 1-19 ARE OVERRULED WITH THE EXCEPTION OF A CLAIM OF ATTORNEY/CLENT PRIVILEGE 2. WITHIN FIFTEEN (15) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER, PLAINTIFF WILL DELIVER TO DEFENDANT MICHAEL D. HUNTERS ALL RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS TO REQUEST NOS 1-19. TO THE EXTENT THAT PLAINTIFF SEEKS TO WITHHOLD ANY RESPONSIVE DOCUMENT CLAIMING ATTORNEY/CLIENT PRIVILEGE, IT WILL IDENTIFY SUCH DOCUMENT WITH SUFFICIENT SPECIFICITY TO ALLOW DEFENDANT TO CHALLENGE THE VALIDITY OF THAT CLAIM. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM: PAUL RENNE, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents
Off Calendar May-07-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MICHAEL HUNTER, MD's Motion To Compel Production Of Documents IS GRANTED AS FOLLOWS:

1. ALL PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO REQUEST NOS 1-19 ARE OVERRULED WITH THE EXCEPTION OF A CLAIM OF ATTORNEY/CLENT PRIVILEGE
2. WITHIN FIFTEEN (15) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER, PLAINTIFF WILL DELIVER TO DEFENDANT MICHAEL D. HUNTERS ALL RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS TO REQUEST NOS 1-19. TO THE EXTENT THAT PLAINTIFF SEEKS TO WITHHOLD ANY RESPONSIVE DOCUMENT CLAIMING ATTORNEY/CLIENT PRIVILEGE, IT WILL IDENTIFY SUCH DOCUMENT WITH SUFFICIENT SPECIFICITY TO ALLOW DEFENDANT TO CHALLENGE THE VALIDITY OF THAT CLAIM.

Order is signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-14-2018 Continued To Nov-14-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Special Anti-Slapp Motion To Strike Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Sep-24-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Trevor Large, Esq. 805-966-7000 of Buynak Fauver Archbald & spray LLP for Plaintiff through Court Call.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MICHAEL HUNTER, MD'S Motion For Leave To File Special Anti-Slapp Motion To Strike Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint.

Defendant Michael Hunter's motion for leave to file an anti-SLAPP motion to strike is denied. Dr. Hunter has failed to show good cause to support his request to file an anti-SLAPP motion at this point in the case. Dr. Hunter did not provide any admissible evidence in his moving papers. The asserted reason for the delay lacks merit. There is nothing improper with calendaring an anti-SLAPP motion against a pro per litigant. Allowing the filing of an anti-SLAPP motion at this stage of the case undermines rather than advances the goals of the anti-SLAPP statute. The prior demurrers and negotiations regarding Dr. Hunter's deposition would have been averted by an earlier anti-SLAPP motion assuming the motion was successful.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Not Reported. =(302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-03-2018 Continued To Oct-03-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-17-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Trevor D. Large, Esq.of Buynak Fauver Archbald & Spray LLP, 805.966.7000, appearing by CourtCall for plaintiff;
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

RULING: The court adopted the tentative ruling which was not contested as to DEFENDANT MICHAEL HUNTER, MD's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Michael Hunter's demurrer urges the Court to resolve several factual disputes, largely based on judicial notice of out-of-court statements. That is not the proper role of a demurrer; rather, "plaintiff's allegations must be accepted as true." (Weil & Brown, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2016) 7:44.) The demurrer is OVERRULED. Order signed. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, Tel. 415.551.5868, Emails: torreanom@aol.com/mtoreano@sftc.org. =(302/RBU)
